channel.title: Set urlBase for theme development
channel.description:
Is there a way to set a `urlBase` to avoid relative paths when developing a theme?
The idea is that for a tree structure like this one...
``` . ├── CHANGELOG.md ├── package.json └── src ├── components │ ├── featured-media.js │ ├── header.js │ ├── index.js │ ├── link.js │ ├── list │ │ ├── index.js │ │ ├── list-item.js │ │ ├── list.js │ │ └── pagination.js │ ├── loading.js │ ├── menu-icon.js │ ├── menu-modal.js │ ├── menu.js │ ├── nav.js │ ├── page-error.js │ ├── post.js │ └── title.js ├── helpers │ └── DataState.js └── index.js
```
You can import from anywhere the component `Title` just by doing ``` import Title from 'components/title' ```
instead of doing ``` import Title from '../title' import Title from './title' import Title from '../../title' import Title from '../../../../title' ```
With `create-react-app` used to be done by setting and environment variable w/ value ``` NODE_PATH=src/ ```
With the latest version of `create-react-app` [you have to create a `jsconfig.json`](https://create-react-app.dev/docs/importing-a-component/) with this...
``` { "compilerOptions": { "baseUrl": "src" }, "include": ["src"] } ```
Related info:
* **https://create-react-app.dev/docs/importing-a-component/#absolute-imports** * https://github.com/facebook/create-react-app/issues/253 * https://github.com/facebook/create-react-app/issues/5645 * https://medium.com/@ktruong008/absolute-imports-with-create-react-app-4338fbca7e3d

item.title: Set urlBase for theme development
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
Customizing Webpack is supported now: Customize Webpack configuration

Read full topic

item.title: Set urlBase for theme development
item.dc:creator: annya.branco
item.description:
Hello!
Any updates on this topic?

Read full topic

item.title: Set urlBase for theme development
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
I have added the tag config-webpack so we can update all the topics that mention this to be able to find them easily once we release this feature.

Read full topic

item.title: Set urlBase for theme development
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
No, that cannot be done right now, but people will be able to do it when we release the frontity.config.js file with support for extending Webpack.

Read full topic

item.title: Set urlBase for theme development
item.dc:creator: juanma
item.description:
Is there a way to set a urlBase to avoid relative paths when developing a theme?

The idea is that for a tree structure like this one…

.
├── CHANGELOG.md
├── package.json
└── src
    ├── components
    │   ├── featured-media.js
    │   ├── header.js
    │   ├── index.js
    │   ├── link.js
    │   ├── list
    │   │   ├── index.js
    │   │   ├── list-item.js
    │   │   ├── list.js
    │   │   └── pagination.js
    │   ├── loading.js
    │   ├── menu-icon.js
    │   ├── menu-modal.js
    │   ├── menu.js
    │   ├── nav.js
    │   ├── page-error.js
    │   ├── post.js
    │   └── title.js
    ├── helpers
    │   └── DataState.js
    └── index.js

You can import from anywhere the component Title just by doing

import Title from 'components/title'

instead of doing

import Title from '../title'
import Title from './title'
import Title from '../../title'
import Title from '../../../../title'

With create-react-app used to be done by setting and environment variable w/ value

NODE_PATH=src/

With the latest version of create-react-app you have to create a jsconfig.json with this…

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "baseUrl": "src"
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}

Related info:

  • https://create-react-app.dev/docs/importing-a-component/#absolute-imports
  • https://github.com/facebook/create-react-app/issues/253
  • https://github.com/facebook/create-react-app/issues/5645
  • https://medium.com/@ktruong008/absolute-imports-with-create-react-app-4338fbca7e3d

Read full topic
